data.body:
RESOLVED 664 code review (4th finding, authoritative-input): verify-opcert now (1) recomputes the AUTHORITATIVE material fingerprint from live ticket state (current_material_fingerprint) instead of trusting the record's self-reported value, and (2) requires the cert's merged_log_commit to be a real ancestor of the gated code HEAD (in-gated-history), fail-closed on git error. Added regression test test_self_reported_stale_material_rejected (self-consistent cert w/ wrong material -> exit 1); mutation-checked (revert to self-reported -> RED). Localized to 664 (verify_opcert.py + 2 tests) — no stack surgery. Rebased local stack onto current origin/main (was 14 behind; Gerrit had rebased to patch set 5) + amended 664; re-pushed all 4 (649/650/660/664). 8 verify-opcert tests pass, lint+typecheck clean. Full make test running.
This means 4214's verify-side authoritative-input concern is now FIXED in-place (not deferred to escalation as I'd earlier framed) — leaving ee0b (produce-side) + 8f1d (producer-key seam) as the escalated infra design.
